context: Tests whether the agent correctly disambiguates inline message moderation as pubnub-functions (per-message edge logic) rather than pubnub-events-and-actions (forward-to-external-system).

checklist.name: Uses handoff template
checklist.description: Output contains all five labeled lines (Intent, Skill, MCP tool, Docs source, Next step) in order

checklist.name: Skill is not pubnub-events-and-actions
checklist.description: The Skill line does NOT name pubnub-events-and-actions (the wrong owner for inline message moderation)

checklist.name: Mentions disambiguation rationale
checklist.description: Either the Intent line or the Next step line clearly characterizes the moderation as inline / per-message / before-delivery (not as an external forward)

checklist.name: Names a concrete MCP tool
checklist.description: The MCP tool line names a real user-pubnub MCP tool (e.g. get_sdk_documentation, how_to, or write_pubnub_app)

checklist.name: Docs source references Functions
checklist.description: The Docs source line references PubNub Functions documentation (e.g. 'Functions 2.0 docs' or 'PubNub Functions docs')

checklist.name: Next step is actionable
checklist.description: The Next step line offers a single, concrete action (e.g. 'Open the pubnub-functions skill' or 'Read references/intent-to-tool.md for the full owner mapping')

checklist.name: No implementation code
checklist.description: Output contains no code blocks implementing the obscenity-replacement logic

checklist.name: Single handoff
checklist.description: Output contains exactly one handoff block, not multiple
